title: Preparation of Testicular Cells for Immunofluorescence Analysis of Manchette in Elongating Spermatids
author: Changmin Niu

steps.title: Isolation buffer Reagent Final concentration Quantity...
steps.instruction: Isolation buffer Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume DNase I 1 μg/mL 5 μL Collagenase IV 0.5 mg/mL 2.5 mg DMEM n/a 5 mL Note: DNase I stock solution is prepared at 1 mg/mL (10 mg of DNase I added to 10 mL of ddH 2 O) and stored at -20 °C. The stock solution needs to be thawed on ice prior to use. It is recommended that the isolation buffer be prepared fresh each time.

steps.title: 1 × PBS Reagent Final concentration...
steps.instruction: 1 × PBS Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume 10× PBS 10% 100 mL ddH 2 O n/a 900 mL

steps.title: Fixation solution Reagent Final concentration Quantity...
steps.instruction: Fixation solution Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume PFA 4% 4 g 1× PBS n/a Add to 50 mL adjust pH to 7.7 with NaOH Sucrose 0.1 M 3.423 g 1× PBS n/a Make up to 100 mL Note: PFA is volatile and unstable; it is recommended to use fresh PFA each time. After preparation, it can be temporarily stored at -80 °C for one month. Before use, the frozen PFA can be thawed in a 42 °C water bath. Place on ice once thawed.

steps.title: Permeabilization solution Reagent Final concentration Quantity...
steps.instruction: Permeabilization solution Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume Triton X-100 1% 10 μL ddH 2 O n/a 990 μL Note: Triton X-100 is stable and can be long-term stored at 2–8 °C under sealed and dark conditions. Triton X-100 appears slightly viscous. To aspirate Triton X-100 easily and accurately, it is recommended to cut the end of the tip.

steps.title: Blocking solution Reagent Final concentration Quantity...
steps.instruction: Blocking solution Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume Goat serum 10% 1 mL 1× PBS n/a 9 mL Laboratory supplies

steps.instruction: 15 mL Falcon tube (Falcon TM , catalog number:

steps.instruction: Pap pen (Scytek Laboratories, catalog number: LP

steps.instruction: Slide (Thomas Scientific, catalog number: 1158B

steps.title: Kim Wipe (Kimberly-Clark, USA)
steps.instruction: Kim Wipe (Kimberly-Clark, USA)

steps.title: Mounting media (VECTASHIELD ® Antifade Mounting...
steps.instruction: Mounting media (VECTASHIELD ® Antifade Mounting Medium with DAPI, catalog number: H-1200-

steps.title: Cover slip (epredia, catalog number:
steps.instruction: Cover slip (epredia, catalog number:

steps.title: Nail polish (Superdry ® )
steps.instruction: Nail polish (Superdry ® )

steps.title: Set up your dissection area in...
steps.instruction: Set up your dissection area in a clean and well-lit animal treatment room/workspace.

steps.title: Put male mice in a suitable...
steps.instruction: Put male mice in a suitable chamber/container for anesthetization using CO 2 as shown in Figure 1A .

steps.title: Ensure the mouse is completely unconscious...
steps.instruction: Ensure the mouse is completely unconscious before taking it out of the container/chamber. This can be confirmed by tapping the mouse directly or shaking the container/chamber gently to monitor movement. Lack of movement is an indication of unconsciousness. Sacrifice mice by cervical dislocation.

steps.title: Place mice with their abdominal side facing upward
steps.instruction: Place mice with their abdominal side facing upward. Use scissors to make an incision to expose the lower abdomen. Pull out the testes and remove the fat and epididymis attached to them. Note: One testis is usually more than enough to get germ cells per experiment.

steps.title: Put the testes in cold PBS to wash
steps.instruction: Put the testes in cold PBS to wash. Then, transfer to fresh cold PBS to remove the membrane (tunica albuginea) surrounding it, as shown in Figure 1D –G.

steps.title: Cut testes into tiny pieces
steps.instruction: Cut testes into tiny pieces. Then, transfer into a 15 mL Falcon tube containing 5 mL of isolation buffer to digest. Figure

steps.title: Stepwise isolation of mouse testis for germ cell retrieval
steps.instruction: Stepwise isolation of mouse testis for germ cell retrieval. (A) Male mice anesthetization in an adequate chamber before cervical dislocation. (B) Abdominal incision to reveal the testes. (C) Isolated testis in cold PBS. (D–F) Removal of the tunica albuginea from the testis before digestion. (G–H) Testicular tissue cut up into pieces to disperse the seminiferous tubules before transfer into isolation buffer. B. Shaking incubation

steps.title: Place the tube in a 32...
steps.instruction: Place the tube in a 32 °C water bath and shake gently (50 rpm) for 8 min. Use a transfer pipette to gently blow tissues off the bottom of the tube every 3 min.

steps.title: Repeat step B1 three more times. Note
steps.instruction: Repeat step B1 three more times. Note: Total time: 4 × 8 min =32 min. By now, the tissues should be completely dissociated, as shown in Figure 2 . Figure

steps.title: Representative image showing well-digested testicular tissues...
steps.instruction: Representative image showing well-digested testicular tissues following 32 min of shaking incubation C. Centrifugation

steps.title: While waiting, cool the Eppendorf TM 5810R to 4 °C
steps.instruction: While waiting, cool the Eppendorf TM 5810R to 4 °C.

steps.title: After four repeats of shaking incubation,...
steps.instruction: After four repeats of shaking incubation, place the tube into the centrifuge and spin down at 200× g for 5 min at 4 °C. D. Discarding supernatant

steps.title: Discard supernatant
steps.instruction: Discard supernatant.

steps.title: Add 5 mL of 1× PBS...
steps.instruction: Add 5 mL of 1× PBS and gently suspend the cells with the transfer pipette.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 200× g for 5 min at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 200× g for 5 min at 4 °C. E. Fixation

steps.title: Add 5 mL of fixation solution
steps.instruction: Add 5 mL of fixation solution.

steps.title: Gently suspend the cells with the...
steps.instruction: Gently suspend the cells with the transfer pipette and shake at room temperature for 15 min on a platform shaker (50 rpm). Note: PFA is toxic and should be handled according to standard safe use practices. Fixation waste should be collected in designated containers adequate for chemicals for hazardous waste cleanup in accordance with institutional environmental and health safety guidelines. F. Examination

steps.title: Centrifuge the cells at 200× g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the cells at 200× g for 5 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Discard the supernatant and wash three times with 1× PBS
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant and wash three times with 1× PBS.

steps.title: Resuspend the cells with PBS at...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the cells with PBS at 1 × 10 5 /mL (1–2 × 10 6 /mL, sperm cells).

steps.title: Add 50 μL of cell suspension...
steps.instruction: Add 50 μL of cell suspension to 500 μL of fresh PBS.

steps.title: Aliquot ~10 μL of cell suspension...
steps.instruction: Aliquot ~10 μL of cell suspension into a Petri dish and examine the cells under a microscope. Note: Ensure the cells are adequately diluted; they should be separated and individualized as in Figure 3A . Figure

steps.title: Microscope view of cell suspension on...
steps.instruction: Microscope view of cell suspension on a slide for density analysis. (A) Representative image showing an adequately diluted germ cell suspension for analysis. (B) Representative image of a slide with too much cell density for analysis. G. Preparation of slides

steps.title: If density is appropriate, use a...
steps.instruction: If density is appropriate, use a PAP pen to draw a square on a positively charged slide.

steps.title: Plate 50 μL of the prepared...
steps.instruction: Plate 50 μL of the prepared cell suspension from F3 onto each square.

steps.title: Gently smear to fill the entire square and leave horizontally
steps.instruction: Gently smear to fill the entire square and leave horizontally. Note: A typical experiment should contain the experimental group, a negative control, and a positive control. The number of slides depends on the type and number of antibodies that are ready to be used. H. Air drying

steps.title: Air dry the cell suspension for...
steps.instruction: Air dry the cell suspension for 45 min to 1 h. Note: Do not let the cell suspension dry completely, as shown in Figure 4 . This is to prevent nonspecific signals. Also, if the samples are too dry, binding of antibodies to the antigen will be impaired.

steps.title: Insufficient drying before performing the next...
steps.instruction: Insufficient drying before performing the next step might lead to cell loss.

steps.title: Optimal dryness is achieved when the...
steps.instruction: Optimal dryness is achieved when the sample area is still wet, but there is no extra layer of liquid on the slide. Figure

steps.title: Representative image of a cell suspension...
steps.instruction: Representative image of a cell suspension that is too dry on the slide I. Permeabilization

steps.title: Place sliders in a wet box ( Figure 5 )
steps.instruction: Place sliders in a wet box ( Figure 5 ). Permeabilize the cells with 50 μL of Triton X-100 (1%) at 37 °C for 5 min. Note: The wet box, also known as a humidity chamber, is a container or device designed to maintain a specific level of humidity within a confined space. Inside the box, there are mechanisms to control and regulate humidity. This can be achieved through the use of water-retaining substances, such as wet sponges or water-filled reservoirs, which release moisture into the air as it evaporates. The wet box is then placed in an incubator at 37 °C. Figure

steps.title: Image of a wet box with slides in it
steps.instruction: Image of a wet box with slides in it. Put slides in the wet box during incubation to prevent the slides from drying out. J. Blocking

steps.title: Wash the cells gently three times with 1× PBS
steps.instruction: Wash the cells gently three times with 1× PBS.

steps.title: Add 50 μL of blocking solution...
steps.instruction: Add 50 μL of blocking solution to each square and incubate the cells at 37 °C for 30 min in the wet box. K. Antibody incubation

steps.title: Gently remove the blocking solution using...
steps.instruction: Gently remove the blocking solution using a 100 μL pipette, add the indicated primary antibodies to the cells, and incubate overnight at 4 °C in a wet box.

steps.title: Gently wash the cells three times with 1× PBS
steps.instruction: Gently wash the cells three times with 1× PBS.

steps.title: Incubate the indicated secondary antibodies in...
steps.instruction: Incubate the indicated secondary antibodies in a wet box at room temperature for 1 h.

steps.title: Gently wash the cells three times...
steps.instruction: Gently wash the cells three times with 1× PBS for a total of 15 min. Gently use a Kimwipe to dry clean the surface of the slide. Notes:

steps.title: Washing is very important and may need to be prolonged
steps.instruction: Washing is very important and may need to be prolonged. Severe washing will make the cells fall off. Washing may be achieved by using a pipette to load PBS onto the slides at the edge of the square, followed by gentle aspiration from the edge to minimize cell loss during washing.

steps.title: Keep in a wet box to...
steps.instruction: Keep in a wet box to prevent slides from drying, and then in a drawer to avoid light and drying. Fluorescent substances tend to experience a gradual decrease in fluorescence intensity when exposed to light, especially intense light. The fluorescent markers used in immunofluorescence experiments, such as FITC (fluorescein isothiocyanate) and TRITC (tetramethylrhodamine isothiocyanate), undergo photochemical reactions under continuous excitation light, which leads to the destruction of their molecular structures and the subsequent loss of their fluorescent properties. If light is not avoided during the experiment, the premature quenching of the fluorescent markers will affect the observation and detection of fluorescent signals, reducing the sensitivity and accuracy of the experiment. L. Mounting

steps.title: Add mounting media (VECTASHIELD® Antifade Mounting Medium with DAPI)
steps.instruction: Add mounting media (VECTASHIELD® Antifade Mounting Medium with DAPI).

steps.title: Slowly cover with a coverslip and seal with nail polish
steps.instruction: Slowly cover with a coverslip and seal with nail polish. Notes:

steps.title: Keep in the dark to avoid light
steps.instruction: Keep in the dark to avoid light.

steps.title: DAPI is a benzimidazole derivative
steps.instruction: DAPI is a benzimidazole derivative. It has a strong affinity for double-stranded DNA due to its ability to intercalate between the base pairs of DNA. It specifically binds to DNA, enabling clear visualization of the cell nucleus and other DNA-rich regions in cells. This high specificity makes it an excellent tool for distinguishing and analyzing the nuclear morphology and distribution of cells.

steps.title: Ensure even distribution of DAPI and...
steps.instruction: Ensure even distribution of DAPI and avoid introducing bubbles during coverage. This can be achieved by adding a drop of DAPI to the center of each sample square, then gently positioning the coverslip to the edge, and gradually tilting down to cover the slide. Then, press down gently for an even spread and the removal of excess DAPI to the side. Remove excess DAPI gently with wipes while ensuring the coverslip is not displaced. M. Imaging Observe and capture images using a fluorescence microscope or a confocal laser scanning microscope.

description: Validation of protocol This protocol or parts of it has been used and validated in the following research article(s): Yap et al. [20]. DNALI1 interacts with the MEIG1/PACRG complex within the manchette and is required for proper sperm flagellum assembly in mice. eLife . ( Figures 4B and 8). Yap et al [18]. MEIG1 determines the manchette localization of IFT20 and IFT88, two intraflagellar transport components in male germ cells. Dev Biol. ( Figure 1A , B) Huang et al. [21]. Autophagy core protein ATG5 is required for elongating spermatid development, sperm individualization and normal fertility in male mice. Autophagy. ( Figure 1B ). Zhang et al. [12]. Intraflagellar transport protein IFT20 is essential for male fertility and spermiogenesis in mice. Mol Biol Cell. ( Figure 5B , C; Figure 6B). Li et al. [22]. A MEIG1/PACRG complex in the manchette is essential for building the sperm flagella. Development. ( Figure 2C , D).
